mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated inflation and restrictive monetary policy compress real disposable income and corporate profit margins, systematically depressing equity valuations over the quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint periods of high CPI and restrictive FEDFUNDS do not produce negative 60-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, driving positive near-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO gains fail to predict positive 5-day returns during identified recovery phases across multiple historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1.3 million units indicate severe aggregate demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as construction and related sectors drag on earnings.
failure_condition: Housing starts remaining below the 1.3 million threshold do not correlate with negative 20-day SPX returns in historical backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over the monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint payroll and retail sales growth periods do not yield positive 20-day returns when controlling for baseline market trends.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarterly prediction window.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns in walk-forward tests, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising policy rates combined with sticky inflation compress valuation multiples and increase financing costs, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rate hikes accompanied by sticky inflation do not produce negative 5-day returns in forward-looking validation periods.
